recipes.title: Amok
recipes.description: Cambodia's national dish of fish steamed in a coconut and kroeung curry mousse, served in banana leaves.
steps: Pound lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, lime leaves, garlic, shallots and chillies into kroeung paste.
Mix kroeung with coconut milk, fish sauce, palm sugar and salt.
Cut fish into 2cm pieces and stir into the curry.
Beat eggs and fold in to enrich the mixture.
Form banana leaves into small bowls or line ramekins.
Place chopped noni leaves in each bowl, then spoon fish curry on top.
Steam over simmering water for 25 minutes until set and fluffy.
Drizzle with extra coconut cream and serve with rice.

recipes.title: Lok Lak
recipes.description: Cambodian stir-fried marinated beef cubes with lime-pepper dip, served on lettuce with rice and tomato.
steps: Cube beef into 2cm pieces.
Marinate beef with garlic, soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce, sugar and 1 tsp pepper for 30 minutes.
Mix lime juice, remaining pepper and salt for the Kampot dipping sauce.
Slice tomatoes, cucumber and onion; arrange with lettuce on a platter.
Heat oil in a wok and stir-fry beef in batches over high heat for 1 minute, keeping the cubes pink inside.
Fry sunny-side up eggs in the same pan.
Pile beef on the lettuce and tomato, top with onions and serve with rice, an egg per person and the dipping sauce.

recipes.title: Nom Banh Chok
recipes.description: Cambodian breakfast rice noodles topped with a fragrant fish-coconut green curry and fresh herbs.
steps: Poach fish in water until cooked, then remove bones and flake.
Pound lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, lime leaves, garlic and shallots into kroeung paste.
Saute kroeung in a little oil until very fragrant.
Add fish, prahok, coconut milk and the poaching water, simmer 15 minutes.
Season with fish sauce and palm sugar.
Cook rice noodles, drain and divide between bowls.
Top with sliced banana blossom, cucumber, bean sprouts and herbs.
Ladle the green fish curry generously over and serve.

recipes.title: Bai Sach Chrouk
recipes.description: Cambodian breakfast of sliced grilled pork over broken rice, served with pickled vegetables and broth.
steps: Slice pork shoulder thinly and marinate with garlic, soy sauce, fish sauce, palm sugar, pepper and coconut milk for 4 hours.
Julienne carrot and daikon, then pickle in vinegar, sugar and salt for 30 minutes.
Cook broken rice as per usual; keep warm.
Grill pork over hot charcoal or in a grill pan until smoky and slightly charred.
Make a simple chicken broth flavored with scallions and pepper.
Fry sunny-side up eggs.
Plate broken rice with sliced grilled pork, pickled vegetables, cucumber, an egg and a small bowl of broth.

recipes.title: Kuy Teav
recipes.description: Cambodian rice noodle soup with pork, shrimp, fish balls and crispy garlic in a clear pork broth.
steps: Parboil pork bones briefly, then drain.
Simmer bones, pork loin, daikon and halved onion in water for 1.5 hours.
Slice the cooked pork loin and reserve.
Season broth with fish sauce and rock sugar, then strain.
Fry minced garlic in oil until deeply golden for crispy garlic topping.
Cook noodles in boiling water and divide between bowls.
Top with sliced pork, shrimp and fish balls, then ladle hot broth over to cook the shrimp.
Finish with crispy garlic, bean sprouts, garlic chives, cilantro and lime.

recipes.title: Cha Houy Teuk
recipes.description: Cambodian iced jelly dessert with coconut milk, palm sugar, jellies and sweet beans.
steps: Boil agar agar with water, pandan juice and sugar until fully dissolved; pour into a tray and chill until set, then dice.
Cook sago pearls in boiling water until translucent, then rinse.
Simmer palm sugar with a splash of water until syrupy, then cool.
Warm coconut milk with a pinch of salt, then chill.
Slice jackfruit into thin strips.
In tall glasses, layer agar jellies, red beans, sago and jackfruit.
Add shaved ice, drizzle with palm sugar syrup and pour over coconut milk before serving.

recipes.title: Samlor Korkor
recipes.description: Cambodian rustic vegetable and meat stew thickened with toasted ground rice and prahok.
steps: Toast rice grains in a dry pan until deep golden, then grind to a coarse powder.
Pound lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, lime leaves, garlic and shallots into kroeung paste.
Brown pork ribs in oil, add kroeung and prahok and cook until fragrant.
Pour in water and simmer 30 minutes.
Add pumpkin and green papaya; simmer 10 minutes.
Add eggplant, long beans and fish; cook 8 minutes more.
Stir in toasted rice powder, palm sugar and fish sauce; simmer until thickened slightly.
Serve with steamed rice.

recipes.title: Prahok Ktis
recipes.description: Cambodian creamy coconut dip with minced pork, fermented fish and chillies, served with raw vegetables.
steps: Pound lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, lime leaves, garlic, shallots and chillies into kroeung paste.
Crack coconut cream in a wok over medium heat until oil separates.
Add the kroeung and fry until very fragrant.
Stir in pork and brown evenly.
Mix prahok with coconut milk and strain into the wok.
Simmer 15 minutes until pork is tender and sauce is thick and oily.
Season with palm sugar and fish sauce.
Serve hot with raw cucumber sticks, long beans and cabbage for dipping.

recipes.title: Char Kreung
recipes.description: Cambodian stir-fry of beef or chicken with kroeung lemongrass paste, long beans and chillies.
steps: Slice beef thinly across the grain.
Pound lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, lime leaves, garlic, shallots and chillies into kroeung paste.
Mix beef with half the kroeung and let marinate 20 minutes.
Heat oil in a hot wok and stir-fry remaining kroeung until very fragrant.
Add beef and sear over high heat for 1 minute.
Add long beans, bell pepper and onion; toss for 2 minutes.
Season with fish sauce, oyster sauce and palm sugar.
Stir in holy basil and serve immediately with rice.
